eventMetadata.context_description: Anthropic commands a 98.5% implied probability as the top coding AI model by April's end, driven by Claude Opus 4.7's April 16 release, which surged 13% on its 93-task internal coding benchmark and cemented a 93.9% lead on SWE-bench Verified—far ahead of OpenAI's GPT-5.x and DeepSeek rivals. markets.question: Will Gold (XAUUSD) hit (HIGH) $4,950 Week of April 27 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during the week of April 27 2026, any 1-minute candle for Gold (XAUUSD) has a final "High" price equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Only prices achieved during the applicable trading session as listed on Pyth will be considered. Under the standard schedule, trading is open from 6:00:00 PM ET Sunday through 5:00:00 PM ET Friday, with a daily break from 5:00:00 PM ET to 6:00:00 PM ET, except where modified by holiday or special-session hours as listed on Pyth.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

If Gold (XAUUSD) does not trade at all during the listed time frame, this market will resolve to "No".

In the event of a contract specification change, feed change, or similar structural modification affecting the market during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the Gold (XAUUSD) "High" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore/Metal.XAU%2FUSD, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter.

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily high price published for the relevant CME COMEX futures contract for the underlying metal—COMEX Gold Futures (GC)—may be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

Watch $4,500 as key support; Friday's U.S. PCE inflation release could trigger volatility before weekly resolution.

markets.question: Microsoft (MSFT) Up or Down on April 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Up" if the Close price for Microsoft Corporation (MSFT) on April 30, 2026 is higher than the Close price for Microsoft Corporation (MSFT) on the most recent prior trading day.

This market will resolve to "Down" if the Close price for Microsoft Corporation (MSFT) on April 30, 2026 is lower than the Close price for Microsoft Corporation (MSFT) on the most recent prior trading day.

E.g., ordinarily, a market on Monday would refer to the previous Friday for its most recent closing price, unless that Friday were a market holiday, in which case it would refer to Thursday, or the next most recent trading day.

If the two specified closing prices are exactly equal, this market will resolve 50-50. Closing prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

If Microsoft Corporation (MSFT) does not trade at all during the regular session, the market will resolve 50-50.

For a standard full trading session, the closing price refers to the Pyth "Close" value of the 1-minute candle corresponding to the final minute of regular trading hours on the primary exchange.

If either of the relevant days has no valid Pyth Close value for the 1-minute candle corresponding to the end of regular trading hours on the primary exchange, the market will use the last valid Pyth price achieved during the regular trading hours of the primary exchange as the effective closing price. If no valid Pyth price exists for that trading day due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption, the official closing price published by the primary exchange on which the listed security trades will be used to determine the closing price for that day.

Only prices achieved during the regular trading hours of the primary exchange on which the listed security trades (typically 9:30 AM – 4:00 PM ET) will be considered.

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed security during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The resolution source for this market will be Pyth, specifically the "Close" values for the relevant 1-minute candle available at https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.MSFT%2FUSD. Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter. Any timestamp within the listed market time frame may be used to view the relevant candle data (e.g., https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.MSFT%2FUSD?t=1773432000).

Verlina Reynolds-Jackson trails closely at 38% bolstered by the Communications Workers of America endorsement on April 9, appealing to union-heavy voters, while mayoral experience positions Adrian Mapp and others like Tennille McCoy and Brad Cohen near 30%, splitting moderate lanes. Recent Princeton and Rider debates highlighted divides on housing, AI regulation, and Israel policy—such as Altman's criticism of Hamawy's Iron Dome stance—keeping the race tight without public polls or county party endorsements; late consolidations, outside spending, or turnout in Mercer and Somerset counties could tip the balance.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices "Yes" at 95.5% for a winless team in the 2026 FIFA World Cup group stage, reflecting the expanded 48-team format's inclusion of low-ranked debutants like Curaçao (FIFA ~82nd, smallest nation ever to qualify), Haiti (~83rd), and New Zealand (~85th), who advanced through late-March intercontinental playoffs and confederation qualifiers alongside hosts USA, Canada, and Mexico. Historical precedent bolsters this—every prior tournament produced at least one 0-win side amid group hierarchies—while recent group draw placements pit these minnows against powerhouses like Bosnia & Herzegovina, Switzerland, and South Africa, heightening loss risks over three matches. Realistic shifts to "No" would require unprecedented parity, with all 48 squads securing a victory via mass upsets or draws.

markets.question: Counter-Strike: Imperial vs Fake do Biru (BO3) - ESL Challenger League South America Cup #4 Playoffs
markets.description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike Upper bracket semifinal 1 match between Imperial and Fake do Biru in the ESL Challenger League South America Cup #4 Playoffs, initially scheduled for April 29 at 5:00PM ET.

This market will resolve to "Imperial" if Imperial win the match against Fake do Biru.

This market will resolve to "Fake do Biru" if Fake do Biru win the match against Imperial.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

markets.question: Will Demeke Mekonnen be the next Prime Minister of Ethiopia?
markets.description:
General elections are scheduled to be held in Ethiopia on June 1, 2026.

This market will resolve to the next individual who officially assumes the office of Prime Minister of Ethiopia following the 2026 General elections.

To count for resolution, the individual must be officially appointed and sworn in as Prime Minister of Ethiopia. Any interim or caretaker Prime Minister will not count toward the resolution of this market.

If no such Prime Minister takes office by December 31, 2028, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the Government of Ethiopia; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus strongly favors incumbent Prime Minister Abiy Ahmed at 86.5% ahead of Ethiopia's general election on June 1, 2026, reflecting Prosperity Party dominance in parliament, where the PM is nominated post-vote, and his unchallenged re-election as PP chair in February 2025. Recent developments, including Abiy's diplomatic push for sea access via negotiations amid Eritrea tensions in March and hosting the AU summit, signal political stability without succession signals. Lower odds on alternatives like Justice Minister Gedion Timothewos, opposition figure Berhanu Nega, and Oromia President Shimelis Abdisa stem from suppressed opposition participation, as highlighted in recent reporting on jailed or exiled rivals and restricted campaigning. Unexpected violence or coalition shifts could alter outcomes, though traders see slim barriers to PP majority retention.

eventMetadata.context_description: Incumbent Mayor Mark Sutcliffe holds a commanding 63% trader consensus in the October 26 Ottawa municipal election market, buoyed by strong name recognition, an established campaign infrastructure, and a fragmented field of challengers that echoes his 2022 victory amid divided opposition. events.title: Which DCMs self-certify sports event contracts by June 30?
events.description:

The primary resolution source will be official information released by the CFTC or the respective DCM; however, a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: A federal appeals court ruling on April 7 affirmed the CFTC's exclusive jurisdiction over sports event contracts traded on designated contract markets, preempting state gambling laws and removing a major barrier for DCM self-certifications. This follows the CFTC's March 12 Staff Advisory 26-08, which encouraged DCMs to engage sports leagues like MLB—now formalized via MOU—prior to listing such prediction market contracts, while highlighting manipulation risks. CME Group has already self-certified offerings, signaling viability amid an advance notice of proposed rulemaking with comments due by late April. Traders eye the June 30 deadline as additional DCMs weigh regulatory clarity against lingering state litigation, potentially expanding legal sports outcome derivatives.

Traders monitor upcoming negotiations and violation trends for escalation risks, with no formal cancellation yet but rising tensions as of late April.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices "No" at a resounding 93.5% implied probability, driven by MrBeast's recent main channel videos consistently falling short of the 50 million first-24-hour views threshold despite massive long-term totals exceeding 100 million. His April 18 upload "Last To Leave Grocery Store, Wins $250,000" sits at 108 million views after 11 days, while the hyped "50 Streamers Fight for $1,000,000" from early April reached 99 million in three weeks—both peaking below 50 million on day one, per prior monthly Polymarket resolutions. This marks a departure from 2023-24 records like 71 million for battle royale challenges, amid YouTube algorithm changes, Shorts competition, and MrBeast's shift to live streams (1 million concurrent viewers) and Amazon's Beast Games. No May teasers signal a record-breaker before May 31, though a surprise high-stakes promo blitz could spark an upset.

markets.question: Will Renan Santos finish in second place in the first round of the 2026 Brazilian presidential election?
markets.description:
A presidential election is scheduled to take place in Brazil on October 4, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the listed candidate who receives the second-most valid votes in the first round of this election.

The named candidates will be primarily ranked by the number of valid votes received in the specified election. If two or more candidates are tied on valid votes, ties will be broken by alphabetical order of the candidates' last names. This market will resolve to the candidate that occupies the second-highest finishing position after applying this ranking.

If the result of this election isn't known definitively by June 30, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the result of the election, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Brazilian government, specifically the Superior Electoral Court (Tribunal Superior Eleitoral, TSE) (e.g., https://dadosabertos.tse.jus.br/).

markets.question: Bill Clinton divorce by June 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Bill Clinton and/or Hillary Clinton announce their intention to divorce by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

An announcement by the specified date of their intention to divorce will be sufficient for a "Yes" resolution, regardless of whether the divorce later actually occurs, or whether the actual divorce occurs outside of the timeframe of this market.

The resolution source will be statements from Bill Clinton and/or Hillary Clinton, and their representatives, however a definitive consensus of credible media reporting may be considered.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors no divorce announcement by former President Bill Clinton or Hillary Clinton before June 30, 2026, reflecting the absence of any public statements, legal filings, or credible reports indicating marital dissolution in recent months. Their 50-year marriage has weathered past scandals, including Epstein-related scrutiny that prompted congressional testimony this week but yielded no divorce signals. With no procedural developments or insider leaks since late-2025 rumors faded, the market's near-certain pricing underscores stability amid advanced age and shared institutional ties. Realistic shifts would require an unforeseen announcement, such as health crises or late-breaking personal revelations, though historical patterns suggest low likelihood.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in London be 18°C on April 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the London City Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 30 Apr '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the London City Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/gb/london/EGLC.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Met Office's latest evening update on April 29 forecasts sunny, dry conditions across London for April 30 under building high pressure, with maximum temperatures of 20-23°C inland per regional guidance for southeast England, though strong easterly winds gusting to 30 mph may temper peaks and create a cooler feels-like temperature around 15°C. Trader consensus favors 19°C at 50.5% implied probability, reflecting caution after April 29's official high settled at 16°C below prior model runs, amid easterly continental air mass delivering clear skies and high UV but moderating warmth through wind shear. BBC aligns at 20°C, yet market-implied odds highlight uncertainty in final afternoon readings at key sites like Heathrow, with 18°C (28%) and 20°C (16.1%) close behind; expect shifts from any overnight cloud tweaks or real-time observations.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors a highest temperature of 20°C (36.5% implied probability) in Munich on May 1, closely trailed by 21°C (22.5%) and 19°C (20.5%), reflecting the latest ensemble forecasts from the German Weather Service (DWD) and ECMWF models projecting daytime highs near 20°C under a mild high-pressure ridge over Central Europe. Recent model runs over the past 48 hours show convergence on these levels after April's average conditions (highs around 13-15°C), with minimal intensification risk due to stable atmospheric patterns and no strong föhn winds from the Alps. Historical early-May maxima at Munich Airport average 17°C, but slight warming trends support the upward skew; expect daily DWD updates and observations to refine probabilities before resolution based on official station data.

markets.question: Will A Just Russia – For Truth (SRZP) win the second-most seats in the next Russian parliamentary election?
markets.description:
Parliamentary elections are scheduled to be held in Russia in September 2026.

This market will resolve according to the political party that wins the second greatest number of seats in the next Russian State Duma election.

If the results are not known definitively by September 30, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The named parties or coalitions will be primarily ranked by the number of seats won in the specified election. If two or more parties are tied on seats, ties will be broken by the total number of valid votes received, with higher vote totals ranking higher. If parties remain tied, ties will be broken by alphabetical order of the listed party abbreviations. This market will resolve to the party that occupies the second finishing position after applying this ranking.

This market's resolution will be based solely on the number of seats won by the named party in the State Duma of the Federal Assembly of the Russian Federation.

This market will resolve based on the results of this election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results reported by Russian government sources such as the Central Election Commission of the Russian Federation.

Note: If a listed party contests the election as part of a jointly listed coalition with one or more parties not listed in this market, the market corresponding to the listed party will represent the coalition and will count all seats won by the coalition collectively. If two or more listed parties contest the election as a jointly listed coalition, with or without additional parties not listed in this market, the market corresponding to the party within that coalition that won the most seats in the previous election will represent the coalition and will count all seats won by the coalition collectively. If such a coalition dissolves, each market will again represent its respective party individually.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent VCIOM polls through mid-April show New People surging to 13.4% in party-list voting intentions—overtaking Communist Party of the Russian Federation (KPRF) at 10.9% and Liberal Democratic Party of Russia (LDPR) at 10.1%—driven by their March congress unveiling a renewal-focused program and a 642% spike in state TV coverage last week. FOM surveys, however, favor LDPR at 10-14% over KPRF and New People, highlighting poll divergence amid managed competition. With the September 20 State Duma election allocating half its 450 seats proportionally and half via single-member districts dominated by United Russia, trader consensus prices KPRF slightly ahead for overall second-most seats, but the tight race could separate on regional candidate strength, further media shifts, or administrative mobilization.

markets.question: Will the price of XRP be between $1.80 and $1.90 on May 2?
markets.description:

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the XRP/USDT "Close" prices currently available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/XRP_USDT with "1m" and "Candles" selected on the top bar.

markets.question: Como 1907 vs. SSC Napoli: O/U 1.5
markets.description:
In the upcoming Serie A game between Como 1907 and SSC Napoli, scheduled for May 2 at 12:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Over" if Como 1907 and SSC Napoli combine to score 2 or more goals in this game.

If the combined total is less than 2, this market will resolve to "Under".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

If the game is started but not completed, this market will resolve according to the official final score published on legaseriea.it. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Como 1907 vs. SSC Napoli - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the Serie A game, scheduled for May 2 at 12:00 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: SSC Napoli, defending Serie A champions and second in the standings with around 66 points after 30 matches, visit surging fourth-placed Como 1907 on May 3 at Stadio Giuseppe Sinigaglia. Como's fairy-tale season features robust home form—nine wins in 16 league games—and unbeaten recent head-to-heads against Napoli, including a 2-1 league victory earlier this term, a 0-0 draw, and a dramatic Coppa Italia quarterfinal penalty win in February. Napoli boast strong away results but grapple with injuries to Romelu Lukaku (muscle, late April), Giovanni Di Lorenzo (knee), Amir Rrahmani (thigh), and Antonio Vergara (foot), potentially disrupting their lineup amid a tight Champions League race. Both enter unbeaten in their last three Serie A outings.

markets.question: Will FURIA win CBLOL 2026 Split 1?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the winner of the Campeonato Brasileiro de League of Legends (CBLOL) 2026 Split 1.

If the 2026 Split 1 is postponed after June 13, 2026 11:59 PM ET, canceled, or a winner has not been declared in this timeframe, this market will resolve to "Other".

If multiple teams are declared winner, this market will resolve in favor of the team whose listed team name comes first alphabetically.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from Riot Games (https://lolesports.com/); however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: LoL: CBLOL 2026 Split 1 Winner
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the winner of the Campeonato Brasileiro de League of Legends (CBLOL) 2026 Split 1.

If the 2026 Split 1 is postponed after June 13, 2026 11:59 PM ET, canceled, or a winner has not been declared in this timeframe, this market will resolve to "Other".

If multiple teams are declared winner, this market will resolve in favor of the team whose listed team name comes first alphabetically.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from Riot Games (https://lolesports.com/); however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: RED Canids' undefeated 6-0 series record and dominant 12-1 game tally through Week 5 lead CBLOL 2026 Split 1 regular season standings, yet trader consensus bunches Fluxo W7M, Vivo Keyd Stars, and RED Canids near 47% implied probabilities, underscoring the razor-thin margins in the single round-robin Bo3 format with one series remaining per team before double-elimination Bo5 playoffs. Fluxo W7M rides a three-series win streak after sweeping paiN Gaming 2-0 last weekend, while Vivo Keyd Stars bolstered their 9-5 game edge with a 2-0 rout of Leviatán; both trail RED by two series but boast superior head-to-head potential and playoff pedigree in past splits. This clustering reflects crowd wisdom on upset risks, macro execution volatility, and top-six advancement paths amid consistent form across the trio.

markets.question: Will NYC Mayor post 200+ posts from May 1 to May 8, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times NYC Mayor Zohran Mamdani (@NYCMayor), posts on X between May 1, 12:00 PM ET and May 8, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies which are recorded on the main feed will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

The resolution source for this market is the "Post Counter" figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

markets.question: US x Iran diplomatic meeting by May 15, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if there is a diplomatic meeting between representatives of the United States and Iran by the listed date, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

A diplomatic meeting refers to a deliberate meeting between representatives of the listed countries who are acting in an official capacity and are authorized to engage in negotiation or diplomacy regarding US-Iranian relations on behalf of their governments. Meetings conducted indirectly, for example, through designated mediators, facilitators, or interlocutors acting with the knowledge and authorization of the relevant governments, will qualify. 

Brief greetings, chance encounters, or talks otherwise not deliberately aimed at diplomacy or negotiation will not count.

The meeting must be in-person (including indirect in-person meetings) and must be publicly acknowledged by either government or reported by a consensus of credible media. Remote meetings, phone calls, or other meetings where the relevant parties are not present will not count.

The resolution sources for this market will be official information from the governments of the United States and Iran, and a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: US-Iran diplomatic efforts remain stalled following the collapse of direct talks in Islamabad earlier this month, where US envoys including Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner met Iranian counterparts amid a US naval blockade of the Strait of Hormuz and fragile ceasefire. In the past 48 hours, the White House has begun reviewing Tehran's latest proposal to end hostilities, reopen the strait, and address nuclear enrichment, though sticking points like Israel's Hezbollah conflict and Iran's enriched uranium stockpile persist. Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi recently met Vladimir Putin in Moscow, blaming Washington for impasse while seeking Russian mediation support. No new in-person meetings are confirmed, but analysts note both sides' incentives to avoid escalation, with potential for indirect channels via Pakistan or Oman to resume soon.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Chicago be between 54-55°F on May 1?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Chicago O'Hare Intl Airport Station in degrees Fahrenheit on 1 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Chicago O'Hare Intl Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/us/il/chicago/KORD.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Fahrenheit (eg, 21°F). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

series.title: Kharg Island oil terminal be hit

eventMetadata.context_description: **Kharg Island oil terminal**, Iran's critical hub handling over 90% of its crude exports in the Persian Gulf, remains undamaged despite US airstrikes on nearby military targets—including bunkers, radar stations, and weapons depots—reported on April 7 amid escalating US-Iran tensions. Official US statements confirm these precision operations deliberately spared energy infrastructure, even as restrikes hit previously targeted sites. President Trump's prior threats to seize or obliterate the facility, coupled with Iran's retaliatory drone and missile barrages against Israel, have heightened risks, yet full storage capacity at Kharg and de-escalation signals via stalled diplomacy have tempered trader expectations for a direct kinetic strike. Upcoming negotiations or Hormuz Strait disruptions could shift dynamics before resolution.

markets.question: Will Anže Logar be the next Prime Minister of Slovenia?
markets.description:
Parliamentary elections are scheduled to be held in Slovenia on March 22, 2026.

This market will resolve to the next individual who is officially elected by the National Assembly (Državni zbor) and sworn in as Prime Minister of Slovenia following the next parliamentary election.

To count for resolution, the individual must be formally sworn in. Any interim or caretaker Prime Minister will not count toward the resolution of this market.

If no such Prime Minister is sworn in by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the Government of Slovenia; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Following Slovenia's closely contested parliamentary elections on March 22, where no party secured a majority and incumbent Prime Minister Robert Golob's Freedom Movement edged out Janez Janša's Slovenian Democratic Party (SDS) with 29 seats to 28, Golob conceded failure to form a coalition government on April 20. This shifted momentum to Janša, whose right-leaning bloc—including potential partners like New Slovenia (led by Anže Logar)—demonstrated commanding support on April 29 by passing an SDS-proposed Government Act reducing ministries from 19 to 14 with 49 votes, exceeding the 46-seat threshold in the 90-seat National Assembly. Trader consensus prices Janša at 90.6% implied probability, reflecting successful coalition negotiations, though a presidential nomination and confidence vote could still introduce uncertainty if talks collapse or external pressures emerge.

markets.question: Will Opendoor (OPEN) close at $5.00-$6.00 on the final day of trading of the week of Apr 27 – May 1?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the official closing price for Opendoor (OPEN) on the final day of trading of the specified week (normally Friday).

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

If the final session of the week is shortened (for example, due to a market-holiday schedule), the official closing price published for that shortened session will still be used for resolution.

If no official closing price is published for that session (for example, due to a trading halt into the close, system issue, delisting, or other disruption), the market will use the last valid on-exchange trade price of the regular session as the effective closing price.

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Yahoo Finance.

The target price will be adjusted proportionally to reflect any stock splits. Resolution will be based on the historical price data as shown on Yahoo Finance after any adjustments have been applied.

The resolution source for this market is Yahoo Finance, specifically the Opendoor (OPEN) "Close" prices available at https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/OPEN/history, published under "Historical Prices."

markets.question: Spread: FC Alverca (-2.5)
markets.description:
In the upcoming Primeira Liga game, scheduled for May 2 at 3:30 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "FC Alverca" if FC Alverca win the game by 3 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "FC Porto".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market will resolve according to the official final score published on ligaportugal.pt. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

eventMetadata.context_description: FC Porto tops the Liga Portugal Betclic table with a commanding lead, setting the stage for a potential title-clinching victory over mid-table FC Alverca in Matchday 32 at Estádio do Dragão on May 2. The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the government of the United States, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

markets.question: Will Apple (AAPL) hit (LOW) $260 Week of April 27 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during the week of April 27 2026, any 1-minute candle for Apple (AAPL) has a final "Low" price equal to or below the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Only prices achieved during the regular trading hours of the primary exchange on which the listed security trades (typically 9:30 AM – 4:00 PM ET) will be considered. Prices occurring during pre-market or after-hours trading will not qualify.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The target price will be adjusted proportionally to reflect any stock splits. Resolution will be based on the historical price data as shown on Pyth after any adjustments have been applied.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the Apple (AAPL) "Low" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.AAPL%2FUSD, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter. Any timestamp within the listed market time frame may be used to view the relevant candle data (e.g., https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.AAPL%2FUSD?t=1773432000)

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily low price published by the primary exchange on which the listed security trades will be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

markets.question: Will the Republicans win the South Carolina Senate race in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the winner of the 2026 midterm South Carolina U.S. Senate election, inclusive of any run-offs.

A candidate shall be considered to represent a party in the event that he or she is the nominee of the party in question. Candidates other than the Democratic or Republican nominee (e.g., Greens, Libertarian, independent) may be added at a later date.

Candidates who run as independents will not be encompassed by the “Democrat” or “Republican” options regardless of any affiliation they may have with the party.

The resolution source for this market is the Associated Press, Fox News, and NBC. This market will resolve once all three sources call the race for the same candidate. If all three sources haven’t called the race in this state for the same candidate, this market will resolve based on the official certification.

Recent consolidation in the GOP primary followed Paul Dans' April 10 withdrawal after challenging Graham, bolstered by President Trump's endorsement and Graham's dominant $11 million cash-on-hand versus rivals like Mark Lynch. June 9 primaries loom as the next catalyst, with historical base rates favoring Republican holds in safe seats like this Cook-rated Solid Republican race.

markets.question: Will Girona qualify for the League Phase of the 2026-27 UEFA Champions League?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed team clinches a league phase spot in the 2026-27 Champions League per UEFA rules. Otherwise, the associated market will resolve to "No".

If at any point it becomes impossible for the listed team to clinch a league phase spot in the 2026-27 Champions League (e.g. they cannot mathematically achieve a Champions League place, cannot qualify through play in European or cup competitions, etc.), the associated market will resolve to "No". 

If the 2025-26 LaLiga season is cancelled, postponed after August 31, 2026 11:59 PM ET, or the teams that qualified for the 2026-27 Champions League have not been officially confirmed by UEFA within that timeframe, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market will be official information from UEFA and/or LaLiga; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

markets.question: Will Russia enter Vasylivka by May 31, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if, according to the ISW map, Russia captures any territory of Vasylivka, Donetsk Oblast, (48.357760° N, 37.038017° E) between market creation and the specified date (ET).

Territory will be considered captured if any part of the specified territory is shaded under a below specified layer on the ISW map (https://storymaps.arcgis.com/stories/36a7f6a6f5a9448496de641cf64bd375) by the resolution date. Otherwise, the market will resolve to “No”.

For any change on the ISW map to qualify for this market’s resolution, the relevant shading indicating Russian control must persist through the next full ISW daily update cycle. If ISW skips a day, shading must persist until the next finalized ISW update is published, regardless of the date. Any continuous shading which reflects either "Assessed Russian Infiltration Areas in Ukraine", “Assessed Russian Control”, “Assessed Russian Advance In Ukraine”, or “Assessed Russian Gains in the Past 24 Hours” will qualify. 

Once a qualifying condition is met, any subsequent loss of control will not be considered towards the resolution of this market.

If Russia comes into control of the specified territory as a result of a negotiated settlement, this will qualify for a 'Yes' resolution, regardless of whether it is shaded red in the ISW map. However, an announcement of a negotiated settlement that gives Russia de jure control will not qualify. Actual control must be established.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the ISW Ukraine map. If the ISW map is rendered unavailable, information from DeepStateMap (https://deepstatemap.live/) may be used. If information from both the ISW and DeepStateMap are rendered permanently unavailable, a consensus of credible reporting may be used.

Note: Any temporary glitches or errors in the map will not be considered.

Ukrainian defenses repelled 52 Russian attacks over the past day near Vasylivka, Udachne, Muravka, and Novopavlivka, amid reports of Russian logistics shortages hampering advances. Incremental gains occurred nearby in Novooleksandrivka last week, yet fortified Ukrainian positions hold the settlement. Traders monitor ISW daily assessments and DeepState maps for territorial changes triggering resolution, with sustained frontline clashes and potential Ukrainian counterstrikes as key variables ahead.

Brian Shortsleeve qualified for the primary ballot with a slim 15.5% of delegate votes, positioning him as the primary challenger at 12.8%, while Mike Kennealy's 14% shortfall ended his path to the ballot, dropping his odds to 1.8% amid reports of campaign suspension. Maura Healey in November.

markets.question: Will Google (GOOGL) close at $340-$345 on the final day of trading of the week of Apr 27 – May 1?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the official closing price for Google (GOOGL) on the final day of trading of the specified week (normally Friday).

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

If the final session of the week is shortened (for example, due to a market-holiday schedule), the official closing price published for that shortened session will still be used for resolution.

If no official closing price is published for that session (for example, due to a trading halt into the close, system issue, delisting, or other disruption), the market will use the last valid on-exchange trade price of the regular session as the effective closing price.

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Yahoo Finance.

The target price will be adjusted proportionally to reflect any stock splits. Resolution will be based on the historical price data as shown on Yahoo Finance after any adjustments have been applied.

The resolution source for this market is Yahoo Finance, specifically the Google (GOOGL) "Close" prices available at https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/GOOGL/history, published under "Historical Prices."

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Sao Paulo be 29°C on April 29?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Sao Paulo-Guarulhos International Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 29 Apr '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Sao Paulo-Guarulhos International Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/br/guarulhos/SBGR.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

events.title: Highest temperature in Shenzhen on May 1?
events.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Shenzhen Bao'an International Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 1 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Shenzhen Bao'an International Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/cn/shenzhen/ZGSZ.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Latest forecasts from the Shenzhen Meteorological Bureau and international models like those aggregated on timeanddate.com project a high temperature of 28°C on May 1, driving trader consensus toward 27°C (36% implied probability) and 26-28°C outcomes amid overcast skies and late showers following recent thunderstorms on April 29. Persistent cloud cover and northeast winds (force 3) are expected to limit solar insolation and peak heating in Shenzhen's subtropical monsoon climate, where early May historical highs average 28-29°C but current transitional patterns introduce cooling after April 30's modest 25°C maximum. High humidity (60-90%) further tempers warming potential, though brief clearing could push toward 28°C; watch daily updates from the China Meteorological Administration for model refinements ahead of resolution.

markets.question: Will Analilia Mejia win the NJ-11 Special Election by 40% or more?
markets.description:
The special election for the seat for New Jersey's 11th congressional district in the U.S. House of Representatives is currently scheduled to take place on April 16, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the margin of victory between the top two candidates in the specified election.

For the purpose of this market, the “margin of victory” is defined as the absolute difference between the percentages of valid votes received by the first-place and second-place candidates. Percentages of the valid votes received by each candidate will be determined by dividing the total number of valid votes each of the top two candidates receives by the sum of all valid votes cast in the election.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher placed bracket.

If two or more candidates tie for the most valid votes in this election, and the tie is between two candidates listed, this market will resolve to the lowest margin bracket for the candidate whose listed last name comes first in alphabetical order. If the tie is between a listed candidate and an unlisted candidate, this market will resolve to the lowest margin bracket for the listed candidate. If the tie is between two or more unlisted candidates, this market will resolve to “Other.”

This market will resolve based on the official vote count once the count has been made official.

If the results of the specified election are not known definitively by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official sources, including https://www.nj.gov/state/.

If a recount is initiated before the vote total has been made official, the market will remain open until the recount is completed and the vote is made official.

eventMetadata.context_description: In New Jersey's 11th Congressional District special election on April 16 to succeed Gov. Mikie Sherrill, Democrat Analilia Mejia's commanding lead of approximately 20-25% over Republican Joe Hathaway—with over 95% of votes tallied—anchors trader consensus at 95.1% odds for that margin, reflecting strong Democratic turnout in this Biden +17, Harris +9 battleground that Sherrill flipped in 2018. Late provisionals flipped Passaic County margins to Mejia and stabilized her edge around 20.7% despite pockets of GOP resistance in moderate Jewish suburbs like Livingston. Final certification awaits remaining mail and provisional ballots, but trends favor continuity; a narrower margin would require an improbable Republican surge in outstanding Democratic-leaning areas.

eventMetadata.context_description: Jannik Sinner's commanding 64% implied probability as top seed stems from his breakthrough Monte-Carlo Masters 1000 clay title two weeks ago, where he dismantled Alexander Zverev in the semifinals and Carlos Alcaraz in the final to reclaim world No. 1 ranking, signaling marked improvement on the surface historically not his strongest. Alcaraz's recent right wrist injury—forcing withdrawals from Madrid, Rome, and defending his Roland Garros title—has cleared a major rival, boosting trader consensus around Sinner's momentum entering the clay-court swing. Zverev at 9.5% reflects his consistent deep runs like Munich quarters and Madrid round of 16, plus strong historical clay record including past French Open finals; Djokovic's 8.8% nods to his three RG titles despite skipping Monte Carlo amid uneven form.

Teammate Michael Olise bolsters Bayern's dominance with 15 goals and 26 assists, earning multiple Player of the Month honors in 2026 and climbing to 17%, fueled by his dazzling right-wing form in UCL knockouts. Kylian Mbappé's 15 UCL goals keep him at 12.5% despite Real Madrid's inconsistencies, while Ousmane Dembélé (11.3%) and Lamine Yamal (8.5%, recently injured) trail amid PSG and Barcelona's solid but less trophy-laden runs; the summer World Cup looms as the ultimate decider.

markets.question: Nothing Ever Happens: Obama
markets.description:

- Obama arrested
- Obama divorce

Otherwise, this market will resolve to “Nothing”.

The full rules for this market can be found here: 
https://polymarket-upload.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/NEH_Obama.pdf

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices "Nothing" at 85% for the Obama market, reflecting the absence of federal charges, arrest, or divorce proceedings against former President Barack Obama since the market opened February 11, with resolution pending through December 31, 2026. No verified legal developments have emerged in the past 30 days, despite unsubstantiated rumors of investigations into Obama-era officials, including DNI Tulsi Gabbard's claims and reports of FBI Director Comey’s indictment. Routine activities, such as Obama's April 17 endorsement of Virginia congressional redistricting efforts and his response to the April 25 White House Correspondents' Dinner shooting, fall outside resolution criteria. Structural and political barriers to prosecuting a former president sustain high implied probability of no qualifying events by year-end.

markets.question: Metamask FDV above $500M one day after launch?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Fully Diluted Valuation of Metamask's token is greater than the value specified in the title 1 day after launch. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No."

The token must be actively, publicly transferable and tradable to be considered a launch.

"1 day after launch" is defined as 4:00 PM ET on the calendar day following launch. The resolution source for this market is the most liquid price source available. If Metamask doesn't launch a token by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

markets.question: Will Tesla, Inc. (TSLA) hit (LOW) $300 in May?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during May 2026, any 1-minute candle for Tesla, Inc. (TSLA) has a final "Low" price equal to or below the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Only prices achieved during the regular trading hours of the primary exchange on which the listed security trades (typically 9:30 AM – 4:00 PM ET) will be considered. Prices occurring during pre-market or after-hours trading will not qualify.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The target price will be adjusted proportionally to reflect any stock splits. Resolution will be based on the historical price data as shown on Pyth after any adjustments have been applied.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the Tesla, Inc. (TSLA) "Low" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.TSLA%2FUSD, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter. Any timestamp within the listed market time frame may be used to view the relevant candle data (e.g., https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.TSLA%2FUSD?t=1773432000)

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily low price published by the primary exchange on which the listed security trades will be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

eventMetadata.context_description: Tesla's Q1 2026 earnings beat expectations with non-GAAP EPS at $0.41 versus $0.37 estimated and revenue of $22.39 billion topping forecasts, yet shares dipped to around $373 post-release due to guidance hiking 2026 capital expenditures above $25 billion for AI, robotics, and autonomy investments, alongside projected negative free cash flow. This tempers trader sentiment on near-term margin pressures despite energy storage growth and Full Self-Driving progress, with analyst consensus price targets averaging $400 amid EV demand headwinds. Polymarket traders weigh May share price thresholds against upcoming delivery reports, robotaxi scaling signals, and macroeconomic risk appetite, as Q2 production ramps could catalyze upside or expose competitive vulnerabilities.

markets.question: Cagliari: Aleksandar Kovacevic vs Marcos Giron
markets.description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Aleksandar Kovacevic and Marcos Giron in the Cagliari, originally scheduled for April 30, 2026 at 10:30AM ET.

This market will resolve to 'Aleksandar Kovacevic' if Aleksandar Kovacevic advances against Marcos Giron.

This market will resolve to 'Marcos Giron' if Marcos Giron advances against Aleksandar Kovacevic.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If the match begins but is not completed, and one player advances due to the opponent's retirement, default, or disqualification, this market will resolve to the player who advances.

If the match ends in a walkover (player withdraws before the start and the other advances automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the ATP Tour. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Marcos Giron enters as the slight trader consensus favorite over Aleksandar Kovacevic in their Cagliari Challenger second-round matchup on outdoor clay, buoyed by his No. 76 ranking versus Kovacevic's No. 97 and a 1-0 head-to-head lead from a 6-3, 7-5 win at 2023 Indian Wells. Giron grinded out a 6-4, 1-6, 6-1 comeback over Aleksandar Vukic in the first round—his first clay win of 2026 (now 1-1 on the surface amid a 13-9 YTD record)—highlighting return prowess. Kovacevic dispatched Luca Potenza 6-3, 7-6(6) but carries a 10-12 season mark after an early peak at No. 54, with just 1-2 on clay. Baseline endurance and serve holds will prove pivotal in this evenly matched American duel.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus positions James Harden as the implied 43.5% favorite for Eastern Conference Finals MVP, reflecting the Cavaliers' status as a top contender in a first-round series tied 2-2 with the Raptors entering Game 5 today, where Harden's playoff debut delivered 22 points and 10 assists alongside all-time scoring milestones against Toronto's Scottie Barnes and Brandon Ingram. Jayson Tatum (24.5%) and Jaylen Brown (20.5%) trail closely as Boston holds a 3-2 lead over the 76ers following Philadelphia's Game 5 upset win (113-97) powered by Joel Embiid's 33 points, with Tatum's playoff averages of 24.8 points, 9.3 rebounds, and 8.5 assists affirming the Celtics' dominance as ECF frontrunners at 47%. eventMetadata.context_description: Vegas Golden Knights command a 59.5% implied probability to win their first-round Stanley Cup Playoffs series against the Utah Mammoth, tied 2-2 after Shea Theodore's overtime tally clinched a gritty 5-4 road victory in Game 4 on April 27. The top-seeded Knights' playoff pedigree—bolstered by their 2023 Stanley Cup triumph—and regained home-ice advantage for pivotal Game 5 at T-Mobile Arena underpin trader consensus, especially after Brett Howden's two goals fueled their comeback from a three-goal deficit. Mammoth goaltender Karel Vejmelka boasts a strong .916 save percentage through three starts, but Utah misses center Barrett Hayton (upper-body injury), tilting matchup dynamics toward Vegas' deeper forward group and power play efficiency in this high-scoring affair.

markets.question: Will Republicanos (REPUBLICANOS) win the most seats in the next Brazilian Senate election?
markets.description:
The next federal Senate election is scheduled to take place in Brazil on October 4, 2026, in which two-thirds of the Senate’s 81 seats will be contested.

This market will resolve according to the political party that wins the greatest number of seats in the next Brazilian Senate election. 

Only seats contested in the next Brazilian Senate election will be considered.

If the result of this election isn't known definitively by June 30, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the result of the election, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Brazilian government, specifically the Superior Electoral Court (Tribunal Superior Eleitoral, TSE) (e.g., www.tse.jus.br/eleicoes/resultados-eleicoes).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors the Partido Liberal (PL) at 79.5% implied probability to win the most seats among the 54 up for election on October 4, 2026, driven by state-by-state polling aggregates and analyses projecting PL to secure 20-22 new senators, expanding its current largest bancada of 15. Recent April projections highlight PL leads in key states like Santa Catarina (Carol De Toni at 30.7%), São Paulo (Eduardo Bolsonaro at 32.4%), and Distrito Federal (Michelle Bolsonaro at 42.9%), bolstered by the April 7 party window migrations including Sergio Moro to PL, strengthening its right-wing bloc amid center parties like PSD and MDB losing most incumbents. NOVO (12.6%) and UNIÃO Brasil (11.1%) trail with competitive showings in southern and southeastern races, such as Marcel van Hattem in Rio Grande do Sul, while PT lags at 2.6% due to weaker Northeast penetration. Upcoming primaries and regional primaries could shift dynamics in this fragmented proportional contest.

markets.question: Will Chicago Sky win the 2026 WNBA Finals?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed team is determined as the champion of the WNBA for the 2026 season. Otherwise, it will resolve to "No".

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed participant to named as the champion of WNBA for the 2026 season per the rules of WNBA (e.g., participant is eliminated), the corresponding market will resolve to "No".

If multiple teams are declared winners, this market will resolve in favor of the team whose listed name comes first alphabetically.

If this event is cancelled, postponed after November 14, 11:59 PM ET, or a champion has not been declared within this timeframe, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source will be official information from the WNBA: however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus reflects intense parity in the 2026 WNBA championship market, with New York Liberty (24.5%), Indiana Fever (21.5%), and Dallas Wings (20.9%) clustered tightly atop implied probabilities due to transformative offseason moves. The April 14 draft elevated Dallas with No. 1 pick Azzi Fudd reuniting alongside Paige Bueckers in the backcourt, while Seattle Storm added international center Awa Fam Thiam at No. 3 and Washington Mystics grabbed Lauren Betts at No. 4, spreading elite rookie talent. Indiana's healthy core of Caitlin Clark, Aliyah Boston, and Kelsey Mitchell fuels Fever optimism after injury-plagued prior campaigns, and New York bolstered their roster with three-time All-Star Satou Sabally. Defending champions Las Vegas Aces re-signed A'ja Wilson but trail amid league-wide elevation from free agency and the April 3 expansion draft for Portland Fire and Toronto Tempo, which redistributed depth and heightened competition across 16 teams.

Indiana's healthy core of Caitlin Clark, Aliyah Boston, and Kelsey Mitchell fuels Fever optimism after injury-plagued prior campaigns, and New York bolstered their roster with three-time All-Star Satou Sabally. Defending champions Las Vegas Aces re-signed A'ja Wilson but trail amid league-wide elevation from free agency and the April 3 expansion draft for Portland Fire and Toronto Tempo, which redistributed depth and heightened competition across 16 teams.

Bass sits at 21% amid persistent challenges like homelessness, crime surges, and infrastructure woes—evidenced by copper wire thefts darkening streets—coupled with polls revealing over 50% unfavorable views and 40% undecided voters in the April 3 UCLA Luskin survey where she led narrowly at 25%. markets.question: Will Al Shabab Saudi Club vs. Al Ittihad Saudi Club end in a draw?
markets.description:
In the upcoming game, scheduled for April 10, 2026
If the game ends in a draw, this market will resolve to "Yes".
Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve to "Yes".

markets.question: Palantir (PLTR) Up or Down on April 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Up" if the Close price for Palantir Technologies Inc. (PLTR) on April 30, 2026 is higher than the Close price for Palantir Technologies Inc. (PLTR) on the most recent prior trading day.

This market will resolve to "Down" if the Close price for Palantir Technologies Inc. (PLTR) on April 30, 2026 is lower than the Close price for Palantir Technologies Inc. (PLTR) on the most recent prior trading day.

E.g., ordinarily, a market on Monday would refer to the previous Friday for its most recent closing price, unless that Friday were a market holiday, in which case it would refer to Thursday, or the next most recent trading day.

If the two specified closing prices are exactly equal, this market will resolve 50-50. Closing prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

If Palantir Technologies Inc. (PLTR) does not trade at all during the regular session, the market will resolve 50-50.

For a standard full trading session, the closing price refers to the Pyth "Close" value of the 1-minute candle corresponding to the final minute of regular trading hours on the primary exchange.

If either of the relevant days has no valid Pyth Close value for the 1-minute candle corresponding to the end of regular trading hours on the primary exchange, the market will use the last valid Pyth price achieved during the regular trading hours of the primary exchange as the effective closing price. If no valid Pyth price exists for that trading day due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption, the official closing price published by the primary exchange on which the listed security trades will be used to determine the closing price for that day.

Only prices achieved during the regular trading hours of the primary exchange on which the listed security trades (typically 9:30 AM – 4:00 PM ET) will be considered.

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed security during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The resolution source for this market will be Pyth, specifically the "Close" values for the relevant 1-minute candle available at https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.PLTR%2FUSD. Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter. Any timestamp within the listed market time frame may be used to view the relevant candle data (e.g., https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.PLTR%2FUSD?t=1773432000).

markets.question: Will the Democratic Party win the IL-09 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the IL-09 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

Longtime Rep. Jan Schakowsky's May 2025 retirement opened the race, but former state Sen. Daniel Biss's March 17, 2026, primary victory over 15 rivals solidified a strong nominee with robust fundraising and local endorsements. Weak Republican opposition, including low-profile nominee John Elleson, reinforces this positioning absent national midterm dynamics. Potential shifts could arise from late scandals, health issues, or a dramatic GOP wave, though historical precedents in D+25 districts suggest high barriers to flipping. The general election is November 3, 2026.

markets.question: Will Elon Musk post 65-89 tweets from April 30 to May 2, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Elon Musk (@elonmusk), posts on X from April 30 12:00 PM ET to May 2, 2026 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies on the main feed such as https://x.com/elonmusk/status/1786073478711353576 will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

Community reposts which are not counted by the tracker not count toward the total.

The resolution source for this market is the 'Post Counter' figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

Lower volumes like <40 sit at 11.5% as traders weigh potential trial fatigue curbing output, with extreme highs dismissed given historical patterns in similar high-profile periods.

markets.question: Will Apple (AAPL) close above $220 end of April?

If the final trading day of the month is shortened (for example, due to a market-holiday schedule), the official closing price published for that shortened session will still be used for resolution.

If no official closing price is published for that session (for example, due to a trading halt into the close, system issue, or other disruption), the market will use the last valid on-exchange trade price of the regular session as the effective closing price.

markets.question: Will Benfica finish 2nd in the Primeira Liga for the 2025-26 season?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed club finishes 2nd in the final standings of the 2025-2026 Primeira Liga season. Otherwise, it will resolve to "No".

In the event of a tie, this market will resolve to the team officially recognized by Primeira Liga as finishing in second place. If multiple teams are officially awarded second place, the market will resolve to the team whose listed name comes first alphabetically.

If at any point it becomes impossible for the listed club to finish 2nd in the 2025-2026 Primeira Liga season (e.g. they are mathematically unable to achieve enough points), the market will resolve to "No".

If the 2025-2026 Primeira Liga season is cancelled, postponed after June 7, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, or there is otherwise no 2nd place team declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from Primeira Liga. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

markets.question: Will "The Great Divide" - Noah Kahan debut week album sales be less than 125k?
markets.description:
Noah Kahan's new album 'The Great Divide' is scheduled to be released on April 24, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the debut week sales for Noah Kahan's album 'The Great Divide', according to Hits Daily Double.

If the album 'The Great Divide' has not been released by May 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to the lowest bracket.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Seoul be 16°C on April 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Incheon Intl Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 30 Apr '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Incheon Intl Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/kr/incheon/RKSI.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

markets.question: Israel x Hezbollah Ceasefire extended by April 26, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if there is an official extension of the 10-day ceasefire agreement between Israel and Hezbollah announced on April 16, 2026, defined as a publicly announced and mutually agreed extension to the halt in direct military engagement between Israel and Hezbollah, by the specified date, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Both extensions of the April 16 ceasefire and new agreements scheduled to take effect before or at the initial agreement's scheduled end will be considered extensions of the ceasefire agreement, provided there is no period during which no ceasefire is in effect.

If a qualifying agreement is officially reached before the resolution date, this market will resolve to “Yes,” regardless of whether the ceasefire extension ultimately takes effect.

An extension of the ceasefire agreement requires clear public confirmation from both the Israeli government and Hezbollah that they have agreed to halt military hostilities against one another for longer than the initially agreed 10-day period, or for an official extension of the ceasefire agreement in place to be otherwise confirmed by an overwhelming consensus of media reporting.

Any form of informal understanding, backchannel communication, de-escalation, or unilateral pause in hostilities without a confirmed agreement on a qualifying extension will not qualify. Similarly, newly agreed-upon humanitarian pauses, limited operational pauses, or temporary tactical stand-downs will not qualify.

A newly agreed-upon broader peace deal will qualify if it includes a qualifying extension of the ceasefire agreement/halt in military hostilities. Agreements that outline future negotiations or de-escalation measures, but do not explicitly commit to extending the ceasefire, will not qualify.

This market’s resolution will be based on official statements from the Israeli government and Hezbollah. However, an overwhelming consensus of credible media reporting confirming that an official ceasefire extension agreement has been reached will suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: A US-brokered cessation of hostilities between Israel and Hezbollah, effective April 17, 2026, for an initial 10 days, was extended by three weeks on April 23 following Washington talks announced by President Trump, aiming to enable broader peace negotiations. However, the truce remains fragile amid mutual accusations of violations: Israel reports Hezbollah drone attacks and rocket fire, prompting airstrikes and evacuation orders in southern Lebanon, while Hezbollah deems the extension "meaningless" without full Israeli withdrawal from a security zone south of the Litani River. IDF Chief of Staff stated as of April 30 there is effectively "no ceasefire" in the area, with ongoing clashes underscoring risks of escalation or further diplomatic efforts before the extension expires around mid-May.

markets.question: LoL: Gen.G vs Kiwoom DRX - Game 1 Winner
markets.description:
This market refers to the LoL match between Gen.G and Kiwoom DRX in the LCK Rounds 1-2, initially scheduled for April 30 at 4:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Gen.G" if Gen.G win Game 1 against Kiwoom DRX.

This market will resolve to "Kiwoom DRX" if Kiwoom DRX win Game 1 against Gen.G.

If the match begins but is not completed, and Game 1 is concluded with a winner determined, this market will resolve based on the completed Game 1. 

If Game 1 is not completed for any reason, this market will resolve 50-50. 

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without play beginning, this market will resolve 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

events.title: LoL: Gen.G vs Kiwoom DRX (BO3) - LCK Rounds 1-2
events.description:
This market refers to the LoL match between Gen.G and Kiwoom DRX in the LCK Rounds 1-2, initially scheduled for April 30 at 4:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Gen.G" if Gen.G win the match against Kiwoom DRX.

This market will resolve to "Kiwoom DRX" if Kiwoom DRX win the match against Gen.G.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: Gen.G holds a commanding edge in the LCK Rounds 1-2 BO3 matchup against Kiwoom DRX, bolstered by a two-series win streak with 2-0 sweeps over DN SOOPers and Nongshim RedForce last week, rebounding to 5-3 overall and 4th in standings after mid-split losses to Hanwha Life Esports and Dplus KIA. Kiwoom DRX sits 8th at 2-6, enduring a four-series losing streak capped by 0-2 defeats to KT Rolster and HLE, though they notched early wins over Nongshim and HANJIN BRION. Gen.G's LCK Cup championship run and lopsided head-to-head history amplify their momentum, while DRX fights for survival amid bottom-half pressure for Road to MSI qualification.
