markets.question: Will Trump and Putin meet next in another EU country?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the location of the next meeting between Donald Trump and Vladimir Putin between September 30 and June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

This market will resolve to "No meeting by June 30" if no qualifying meeting occurs during this market's timeframe.

A meeting is defined as any encounter where Putin and Trump are all present and interact with each other in person.

An exchange of words, handshake, direct conversation, or other clear personal interaction between the named individuals will qualify as a meeting. Merely standing in proximity, making eye contact, or being present in the same room or event without direct interaction will not qualify.

For this market, Gulf states are defined as the six members of the Gulf Cooperation Council (Bahrain, Kuwait, Oman, Qatar, Saudi Arabia, United Arab Emirates).

markets.question: Will UK GDP growth in Q1 2026 be between 0% and 0.3%?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the UK real gross domestic product (GDP) growth rate (% change) for Q1 of 2026 compared with the same quarter a year ago in the "GDP First quarterly estimate, UK" release for Q1 of 2026, scheduled for May 14, 2026.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

The GDP release will be made available here: https://www.ons.gov.uk/economy/grossdomesticproductgdp/bulletins/gdpfirstquarterlyestimateuk/previousreleases

If no data for the specified quarter is released by the date the next quarter's data is scheduled to be released, this market will resolve based on data from the last available quarter.

Note: data from the initial release of the referenced GDP report is what will be used to resolve this market. Data may be revised during the following quarter or as a part of the next estimate's publication, however any revisions to GDP report data made after the initial release will not be considered for this market's resolution.

No major injuries or suspensions alter lineups, heightening the competitive table clash with Al Qadisiyah's away challenges and end-of-season stakes.

markets.question: UFC Fight Night: Dom Mar Fan vs. Kody Steele (Lightweight, Prelims)
markets.description:

eventMetadata.context_description: Dom Mar Fan enters his official UFC debut on a five-fight win streak, fresh off a unanimous decision victory in the Road to UFC Season 4 lightweight finals on January 31, capping tournament wins that honed his striking (56% accuracy, 3.71 SLpM) and takedown defense (66%). The Brisbane native holds a four-inch reach edge over Kody Steele and benefits from home-crowd energy at Perth's RAC Arena. Steele, a BJJ black belt seeking redemption after a unanimous decision loss to Rongzhu in his February 2025 UFC debut—his only pro defeat—faces a 15-month layoff, travel demands to Australia again, and stylistic pressure from Fan's versatile game, including recent interviews highlighting both fighters' explosive exchanges and calculated adjustments. No confirmed injuries or weigh-in issues reported as of April 29.

markets.question: LoL: BIG vs Team Orange Gaming (BO3) - Prime League 1st Division Group A
markets.description:
This market refers to the LoL match between BIG and Team Orange Gaming in the Prime League 1st Division Group A, initially scheduled for April 30 at 11:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "BIG" if BIG win the match against Team Orange Gaming.

This market will resolve to "Team Orange Gaming" if Team Orange Gaming win the match against BIG.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: Berlin International Gaming tops Prime League Spring Phase 1 standings with a perfect 9-0 map record and 27 points, entering Group A Phase 2 undefeated after 2-0 sweeps over Kaufland Hangry Knights and ROSSMANN Centaurs last week. TeamOrangeGaming, 6th overall at 5-4 maps (15 points) in Phase 1, matches BIG's 1-0 series start in Phase 2 via identical wins against those bottom feeders. BIG owns the head-to-head edge, blanking TOG 2-0 in Winter playoffs quarters. Stable rosters post-January overhauls make this BO3 crucial for group lead, playoff seeding, and EMEA Masters path, highlighting a competitive clash driven by recent momentum.

eventMetadata.context_description: Jay Feely leads trader consensus at 70% implied probability in the AZ-01 Republican primary for the open seat vacated by David Schweikert, bolstered by President Trump's January dual endorsement—shared initially with Gina Swoboda—and recent backing from Speaker Mike Johnson, House GOP leadership, and Rep. Juan Ciscomani on April 28. Swoboda's February pivot toward the GOP secretary of state nomination has consolidated support behind Feely, elevating him as frontrunner despite a late April poll showing Joseph Chaplik ahead 24% to 15% among likely voters amid high undecideds. Chaplik, who resigned from the state legislature in February to focus full-time, appeals to Freedom Caucus conservatives but lacks Trump's nod. The August primary remains fluid with endorsements and fundraising key amid battleground district dynamics.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors state Rep. Jasmine Clark at 80.5% to win Georgia's 13th Congressional District Democratic primary on May 19, propelled by the April 22 death of longtime incumbent David Scott, whose name remains on the ballot but trades at just 1%. Clark's superior fundraising—over $1 million raised and $482,000 cash on hand as of late March—along with her prior polling tie against Scott and established campaign infrastructure in the safe Democratic district, drove a sharp post-death surge in her odds, bolstered by super PAC spending exceeding $1 million. Former Gwinnett school board member Everton Blair Jr. holds second at 16% on strong grassroots support and $644,000 raised, while early voting set records this week amid no new public polls.

markets.question: Will Doja Cat have a #1 song on the Billboard Hot 100 in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if any song by the listed artist is the number 1 song in any official weekly Billboard Hot 100 songs chart released for a data collection reference period entirely between January 1 and December 31, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Any song released with the listed artist as a primary artist will count, including songs released before 2026. If a song is released jointly by multiple artists credited equally as primary artists, it will count for all of them. Features, writing credits, production credits, or other forms of non-primary contributions to a song will not count. Artist attribution will be determined solely by the artists displayed by Billboard on the relevant chart.

Billboard updates its Hot 100 songs chart each Tuesday (with adjusted release schedules on some holiday weeks), reflecting data collected in the previous week (Friday-Thursday). Each Billboard chart is then titled “Week of (date of the upcoming Saturday)”. The first Billboard Hot 100 song chart relevant to this market will be the chart titled “Week of January 17, 2026” for a data collection reference period of January 2-8, 2026. The final Billboard Hot 100 song chart relevant to this market will be the Chart titled “Week of January 9, 2027” for a data collection reference period of December 25-31, 2026.

Updates or revisions made within this market’s timeframe to the #1-ranked song on previously published Billboard Hot 100 charts will be considered; however, such updates will not disqualify a previously published Billboard Hot 100 #1 song from counting. If a Billboard Hot 100 #1 song is revised after initial publication, both the originally published #1 song and the revised #1 song will count for this market. Updates or revisions made after the release of the final Billboard Hot 100 song chart relevant to this market will not be considered.

The resolution source for this market will be the weekly Billboard Hot 100 songs charts published each week at https://www.billboard.com/charts/hot-100/ and through other official Billboard channels.

eventMetadata.context_description: Ella Langley's "Choosin' Texas" has solidified its frontrunner status as 2026's longest-running Billboard Hot 100 #1 with seven weeks atop the chart, fueled by massive streaming gains on Spotify and Apple Music alongside country-pop crossover appeal. So far, eight artists have claimed the summit: Olivia Rodrigo ("Drop Dead"), BTS ("Swim"), Bruno Mars ("I Just Might"), Taylor Swift ("Opalite"), Bad Bunny ("DtMF"), and others, reflecting diverse genre momentum from K-pop to Latin trap. Nicki Minaj recently emerged as the highest-charting rapper at #11 peak, dominating global platforms as the first hip-hop act to hit #1 there this year without new music. Traders eye summer releases from heavyweights like Drake amid weekly chart volatility driven by sales, airplay, and viral TikTok trajectories, with eight months left for shifts.

markets.question: Will Mexico GDP growth in Q1 2026 be between 0.5% and 1.0%?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to Mexico's Y/Y Growth Rate of Gross Domestic Product (GDP) in the "Timely Estimate of Quarterly GDP" release for Q1 of 2026, scheduled for release on April 30, 2026.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

The GDP release will be made available here: https://en.www.inegi.org.mx/app/saladeprensa/

If no data for the specified quarter is released by the date the next quarter's data is scheduled to be released, this market will resolve based on data from the last available quarter.

Note: data from the initial release of the referenced GDP report is what will be used to resolve this market. Data may be revised during the following quarter or as a part of the next estimate's publication, however any revisions to GDP report data made after the initial release will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices an 82.8% implied probability for Mexico Q1 2026 GDP growth below 1.0%, with the 0.0-0.5% bin leading at 47.8%, reflecting January's sharp 0.9% monthly IGAE contraction—the steepest in a year—and subsequent stagnation amid declining gross fixed investment and private consumption. Soft manufacturing PMI readings (47.4 in March, signaling contraction) and persistent industrial weakness have anchored expectations for tepid quarterly expansion around 0.2-0.4% annualized, offsetting modest services resilience. Banxico's surprise 25 basis point rate cut to 6.75% in late March signals easing policy amid cooling inflation to 4.53% in early April, but USMCA review uncertainties cap upside. INEGI's timely Q1 GDP estimate releases April 30, potentially confirming or shifting this low-growth positioning.

markets.question: Will Sheffield Wednesday FC vs. West Bromwich Albion FC end in a draw?
markets.description:
In the upcoming game, scheduled for May 2, 2026
If the game ends in a draw, this market will resolve to "Yes".
Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve to "Yes".
This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

eventMetadata.context_description: West Bromwich Albion's trader consensus at 63.5% reflects their unbeaten run over the last 10 Championship matches, including five straight clean sheets, and recent confirmation of mid-table safety in 21st place after a goalless draw against promotion-chasing Ipswich Town. Sheffield Wednesday, rooted to the bottom of the table on -3 points from heavy deductions and already relegated, sit at just 16% amid a winless streak spanning 39 league games and no home victory at Hillsborough in over a year, exacerbated by midfielder Svante Ingelsson's confirmed knee absence announced yesterday. West Brom's superior head-to-head record and defensive solidity underpin the pricing, though their potential rotation on the final day leaves room for a competitive draw priced at 20.5%.

markets.question: Will the Republican Party win the PA-06 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the PA-06 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Incumbent Democrat Chrissy Houlahan's strong reelection bid in Pennsylvania's 6th Congressional District drives the 92.5% trader consensus favoring the Democratic Party, reflecting her consistent 56% general election margins since 2018, superior fundraising with $3.9 million cash on hand versus Republican Marty Young's $176,000, and the district's D+6 partisan lean encompassing Democratic-trending Chester County suburbs. Rated Solid Democratic by Cook Political Report and others, PA-06 lacks competitive polling or recent catalysts, with Houlahan facing no viable Democratic primary opponent ahead of the May 19 contest and Young as the sole GOP filer showing limited traction. Scenarios to shift odds include a Houlahan scandal, health issue, or extraordinary national Republican midterm wave overcoming her biographical appeal as an Air Force veteran and moderate.

markets.question: Will Microsoft (MSFT) close above $435 end of April?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the official closing price for Microsoft Corporation (MSFT) on the final trading day of April 2026 is higher than the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If the final trading day of the month is shortened (for example, due to a market-holiday schedule), the official closing price published for that shortened session will still be used for resolution.

If no official closing price is published for that session (for example, due to a trading halt into the close, system issue, or other disruption), the market will use the last valid on-exchange trade price of the regular session as the effective closing price.

The resolution source for this market is Yahoo Finance — specifically, the Microsoft Corporation (MSFT) "Close" prices available at https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/MSFT/history, published under "Historical Prices."

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Yahoo Finance.

eventMetadata.context_description: The open seat in New York's 21st Congressional District after Rep. Elise Stefanik's decision not to seek reelection has fueled a competitive Republican primary, with President Trump's April 21 endorsement of Anthony Constantino—over state party favorite Assemblyman Robert Smullen—creating intra-party tension and fundraising battles that traders view as potential vulnerabilities. Constantino leads in cash and internal polling from Stefanik's pollster, bolstering the Republican edge in this R+10 leaning battleground encompassing rural North Country areas. Democrats lack a clear frontrunner ahead of the June 23 primaries, yet trader consensus at 47% Republican versus 39% Democratic reflects uncertainty from the GOP contest and national midterm dynamics, with post-primary consolidation or polling shifts poised to widen the gap.

markets.question: Will UBS or any of its underwriting affiliates serve as the lead underwriter in SpaceX’s initial public offering?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the bank that serves as the lead underwriter in the initial public offering of SpaceX.

If no IPO occurs by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, or SpaceX completes an initial public offering without a designated lead underwriter, this market will resolve to “Other.”

If multiple banks are identified as lead underwriters, this market will resolve according to the primary lead underwriter. If the hierarchy between them is unclear, this market will resolve once it is conclusively evident which bank is the primary lead underwriter, for example, through the order in which the banks are listed in the underwriting section of SpaceX’s final initial public offering prospectus, once released.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official disclosures from SpaceX. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors Morgan Stanley as lead bank for SpaceX's anticipated 2026 IPO, with 54% implied probability, driven by its longstanding ties to Elon Musk—including banker Michael Grimes' return and prior role in Tesla's IPO—alongside reports of leading the final bake-off at Starbase. Bank of America follows at 24%, bolstered by its inclusion in the core group of five active bookrunners (with Goldman Sachs at 13%, JPMorgan, and Citigroup) confirmed during SpaceX's April 6 virtual meeting with 21 syndicate banks for "Project Apex," targeting a $1.75 trillion valuation and $75 billion raise. Recent jockeying between Morgan Stanley and Goldman Sachs underscores competitive dynamics, but no single "lead left" role is expected, with banks listed alphabetically; watch for the late-May prospectus and June 8 roadshow for resolution clarity.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Wuhan be 23°C on May 1?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Wuhan Tianhe International Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 1 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Wuhan Tianhe International Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/cn/wuhan/ZHHH.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket closely splits between 24°C and 25°C for Wuhan's highest temperature on May 1, reflecting the Chinese Meteorological Administration's latest forecast of 25°C under mostly cloudy skies at Wuhan Tianhe International Airport. This positioning stems from a late-April cool air mass that delivered an unusually low 8°C high today (April 29), followed by a rebound to 23°C tomorrow amid light northerly winds and partial solar heating. Differentiating factors include cloud cover variability—thicker overcast from mid-level moisture could cap peaks at 24°C, while breaks might allow 25°C—consistent with GFS and ECMWF ensemble means near 24-25°C. Updated model runs expected tonight may refine this tight race before resolution.

markets.question: Le Mans FC vs. Stade de Reims: O/U 2.5
markets.description:
In the upcoming Ligue 2 game between Le Mans FC and Stade de Reims, scheduled for May 2 at 2:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Over" if Le Mans FC and Stade de Reims combine to score 3 or more goals in this game.

If the combined total is less than 3, this market will resolve to "Under".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

If the game is started but not completed, this market will resolve according to the official final score published on ligue2.fr. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Le Mans FC vs. Stade de Reims - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the Ligue 2 game, scheduled for May 2 at 2:00 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Le Mans FC, entrenched in third place on the Ligue 2 table with 15 wins and robust home form (eight victories at Stade Marie-Marvingt), host fourth-placed Stade de Reims on May 2 in a pivotal promotion six-pointer. Le Mans boast momentum from recent triumphs including a 4-0 rout of Pau FC on April 6 and a 4-3 comeback at Amiens, bolstering their clean-sheet streak. Reims remain competitive but stumbled to a 2-2 draw at Laval last week, compounded by off-field disruption from armed fan threats at their training ground five days ago potentially impacting morale. Reims dominate head-to-head (unbeaten in six, including February's 3-0 Coupe de France win), though Le Mans contend without long-term absentee Jean Vercruysse (cruciate ligament tear).

markets.question: Will Valtteri Bottas be the 2026 F1 Drivers' Champion?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the listed driver that finishes 1st in the driver standings for the 2026 F1 season.

This market will resolve as soon as the official results of the final scheduled race of the 2026 F1 season are known.

If multiple drivers tie for first place in the drivers standings, this market will resolve according to the tiebreak procedure used by F1 to determine the 2026 F1 Drivers’ champion.

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed driver to win the 2026 F1 Drivers Championship based on the rules of F1 (e.g., they are mathematically eliminated from contention), the corresponding market will resolve to “No”.

If the F1 season is permanently canceled or has not been completed by March 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Formula 1.

eventMetadata.context_description: Mercedes' dominant start to the 2026 season, highlighted by a 1-2 finish at the Australian Grand Prix where George Russell won ahead of teammate Kimi Antonelli, has positioned their drivers as overwhelming title favorites in trader consensus. Antonelli's breakout wins in China and Japan—securing pole in Suzuka and extending his drivers' lead to 72-63 points—have fueled his 31.1% implied probability, matching Ayrton Senna's rare feat of winning after a poor opening stint. Yet Russell edges at 41.5% on experience in prolonged title fights, with the squad's 135-point constructors' lead over Ferrari's 90 dwarfing challengers like Charles Leclerc (6.6%) amid Bahrain and Saudi cancellations delaying Miami scrutiny.

eventMetadata.context_description: Hong Kong Observatory's latest forecast, updated early on April 30, projects a maximum temperature around 26°C under mainly cloudy skies with sunny intervals, aligning trader consensus at 79.5% implied probability for 25°C or higher amid a northeast monsoon ushering cooler continental air after yesterday's 28.4°C peak. This subtropical region's late-April climatology—averaging highs near 27°C—supports daytime solar heating rebound despite cooler mornings 2–4°C below prior norms and 50–80% humidity. The 14.5% odds on exactly 24°C reflect uncertainty from potential persistent cloud bands capping intensity, while sub-25°C outcomes (<6% total) defy historical baselines and model runs. Hourly HKO observations will refine trajectories as the day unfolds.

markets.question: Will Ted Cruz post 200+ posts from April 28 to May 5, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Ted Cruz (@tedcruz), posts on X between April 28, 12:00 PM ET and May 5, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies which are recorded on the main feed will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

The resolution source for this market is the "Post Counter" figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Sen. Ted Cruz's (@tedcruz) X posts from April 28 to May 5 tightly clusters in the 60-139 range, mirroring his consistent weekly volume of 80-120 observed in recent resolved markets—such as April 10-17 landing in 80-99 and April 21-28 favoring 100-119 amid an active Senate calendar. This close contest stems from predictable variability tied to floor sessions resuming April 28 with caucus meetings, potential Commerce Committee hearings under Cruz's chairmanship, and reactions to policy debates on trade or oversight. Separation could arise from intensified legislative votes, breaking foreign policy news prompting commentary, or conversely, lighter schedules allowing fewer posts below 60.

markets.question: Will the Republican Party win the SC-02 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the SC-02 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors the Republican Party at 82% to win South Carolina's 2nd Congressional District House seat, reflecting the district's R+7 partisan voting index, Donald Trump's 56%-42% margin in 2024, and Solid Republican ratings from Cook Political Report, Inside Elections, and Sabato's Crystal Ball. Incumbent Joe Wilson dominates the June 9 Republican primary field against challengers Sam Gibbons and Hamp Redmond, boasting $669,000 in fundraising through March 31—far outpacing rivals—fueling uncertainty among specific candidates labeled A, B, and Other near 50%. Democrats trail at 17.5% amid a crowded primary of Zyon Khalifa, Roger Pruitt, David Robinson II, and Daniel Shrief with negligible cash on hand post-March 30 filing deadline, alongside Workers Party's Dayna Alane Smith. Primaries on June 9 and general election November 3 loom as key catalysts.
